description: Two imprisoned men bond over a number of years, finding solace and eventual redemption through acts of common decency.
review: An absolute masterpiece of cinema. The storytelling, acting, and message of hope are unmatched.

description: The aging patriarch of an organized crime dynasty transfers control of his clandestine empire to his reluctant son.
review: The greatest crime movie ever made. Flawless acting by Marlon Brando and Al Pacino.

description: When the menace known as the Joker wreaks havoc and chaos on the people of Gotham, Batman must accept one of the greatest psychological and physical tests.
review: Ledger's Joker is legendary. A dark, intense, and brilliant comic book adaptation.

description: The early life and career of Vito Corleone in 1920s New York City is portrayed, while his son, Michael, expands and tightens his grip on the family crime syndicate.
review: A rare sequel that matches, if not surpasses, the brilliant original masterpiece.

description: The jury in a New York City boy's mutiny trial is frustrated by a single member whose skeptical caution forces them to more carefully consider the evidence.
review: An incredible study of human psychology and tension inside a single room.

description: In German-occupied Poland during World War II, industrialist Oskar Schindler gradually becomes concerned for his Jewish workforce after witnessing their persecution.
review: Heartbreaking, powerful, and essential viewing. Spielberg at his absolute best.

movie_title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
description: Gandalf and Aragorn lead the World of Men against Sauron's army to draw his gaze from Frodo and Sam as they approach Mount Doom with the One Ring.
review: The perfect conclusion to an epic trilogy. The battle scenes are breathtaking.

description: The lives of two mob hitmen, a boxer, a gangster and his wife, and a pair of diner bandits intertwine in four tales of violence and redemption.
review: Tarantino's dialogue is iconic and sharp. Completely changed modern cinema.

description: A thief who steals corporate secrets through the use of dream-sharing technology is given the inverse task of planting an idea into the mind of a C.E.O.
review: Mind-bending visuals and a stunning, complex narrative. Absolutely gripping.

description: An insomniac office worker and a devil-may-care soap maker form an underground fight club that evolves into much more.
review: Edgy, thought-provoking, and features one of the greatest plot twists in film history.

description: The history of the United States from the 1950s to the 1970s unfolds from the perspective of an Alabama man with an IQ of 75.
review: A beautiful, heartwarming journey through history with an unforgettable performance by Tom Hanks.

description: When a beautiful stranger leads computer hacker Neo to a forbidding underworld, he discovers the shocking truth--the life he knows is the elaborate deception of an evil cyber-intelligence.
review: Groundbreaking special effects and philosophical themes that redefined the sci-fi genre.

description: The story of Henry Hill and his life in the mob, covering his relationship with his wife Karen Hill and his mob partners Jimmy Conway and Tommy DeVito.
review: High energy, incredible soundtrack, and masterful direction by Martin Scorsese.

movie_title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
description: In the Fall of 1963, a Korean War veteran and criminal pleads insanity and is admitted to a mental institution, where he rallies the patients against an oppressive nurse.
review: Jack Nicholson is electric. A profound, rebellious masterpiece against authoritarian control.

description: Two detectives, a rookie and a veteran, hunt a serial killer who uses the seven deadly sins as his motives.
review: Dark, atmospheric, and unforgettable. The ending leaves you completely shocked.

description: When Earth becomes uninhabitable, a team of explorers travels through a wormhole in space in an attempt to ensure humanity's survival.
review: An emotional and visual masterpiece. Hans Zimmer's musical score is legendary.

description: A young F.B.I. cadet must receive the help of an incarcerated and manipulative cannibal killer to help catch another serial killer.
review: Anthony Hopkins gives a chilling performance. A masterful psychological thriller.

description: Following the Normandy Landings, a group of U.S. soldiers go behind enemy lines to retrieve a paratrooper whose brothers have been killed in action.
review: The opening beach landing sequence is the most realistic and visceral war scene ever filmed.

description: During her family's move to the suburbs, a sullen 10-year-old girl wanders into a world ruled by gods, witches, and spirits, and where humans are changed into beasts.
review: A magical, visually spectacular anime that appeals deeply to both adults and kids.

description: A tale about the lives of guards on death row leading up to the execution of a giant man accused of child murder and rape, who has a mysterious gift.
review: A deeply emotional and spiritual film. It brings tears to my eyes every single time.

movie_title: Star Wars: Episode V - The Empire Strikes Back
description: After the Rebels are brutally overpowered by the Empire on the ice planet Hoth, Luke Skywalker begins Jedi training with Yoda.
review: The ultimate Star Wars film. Richer character development and a legendary dark twist.

description: Greed and class discrimination threaten the newly formed symbiotic relationship between the wealthy Park family and the destitute Kim clan.
review: A phenomenal social satire with brilliant pacing, dark humor, and unpredictable turns.

description: A promising young drummer enrolls at a cut-throat music conservatory where his dreams of greatness are mentored by an instructor who will stop at nothing.
review: Intense, thrilling, and displays an incredible clash of wills. The finale is stunning.

description: After a tragic accident, two stage magicians in 1890s London engage in a battle to create the ultimate illusion while sacrificing everything they have to outwit each other.
review: A beautifully constructed puzzle box of a movie that demands multiple viewings.

description: Lion prince Simba and his father are targeted by his bitter uncle, who wants to ascend the throne himself.
review: The pinnacle of Disney traditional animation. Beautiful songs and a timeless story.

description: A former Roman General sets out to exact vengeance against the corrupt emperor who murdered his family and sent him into slavery.
review: An epic tale of vengeance and honor with a powerful, commanding performance by Russell Crowe.

description: An undercover cop and a mole in the police attempt to identify each other while infiltrating an Irish gang in South Boston.
review: A stellar cast and razor-sharp direction make this an outstanding cat-and-mouse thriller.

description: A Polish Jewish musician struggles to survive the destruction of the Warsaw ghetto during World War II.
review: A deeply moving, raw, and authentic portrayal of survival amidst historical horror.

description: Marty McFly, a 17-year-old high school student, is accidentally sent thirty years into the past in a time-traveling DeLorean invented by his close friend, Doc Brown.
review: The perfect screenplay. Delightful, energetic, and infinitely rewatchable.

description: A cyborg, identical to the one who failed to kill Sarah Connor, must now protect her ten-year-old son John from a more advanced and powerful cyborg.
review: The benchmark for action cinema. Exceptional practical and digital visual effects.

description: The crew of a commercial spacecraft encounter a deadly lifeform after investigating an unknown transmission.
review: A brilliant blend of sci-fi tech and claustrophobic horror. Truly terrifying.

description: A Phoenix secretary embezzles $40,000 from her employer's client, goes on the run, and checks into a remote motel run by a young man under the domination of his mother.
review: Hitchcock at his most devious. It redefined the horror and thriller genres forever.

description: With the help of a German bounty-hunter, a freed slave sets out to rescue his wife from a brutal Mississippi plantation owner.
review: Stylized, bold, highly entertaining, and packed with brilliant performances.

description: Teen Miles Morales becomes the Spider-Man of his universe and must join with five spider-powered individuals from other dimensions to stop a threat.
review: The most visually creative animation style ever seen. Fresh, fun, and emotionally impactful.

description: A man with short-term memory loss attempts to track down his wife's murderer.
review: Nolan's structural mastery tells the story backward to put you right in the protagonist's shoes.

description: The origins of a failed comic who turns to a life of crime and chaos in Gotham City.
review: Joaquin Phoenix delivers an extraordinary, disturbing, and character-driven psychological study.

description: Two strangers find themselves linked in a bizarre way. When a connection forms, will distance be the only thing to keep them apart?
review: Stunningly gorgeous animation combined with an emotionally powerful, supernatural love story.

description: A family heads to an isolated hotel for the winter where a sinister presence influences the father into violence.
review: Kubrick's masterpiece of atmospheric horror. Every frame feels unnerving and deeply ominous.

description: Aspiring musician Miguel, confronted with his family's ancestral ban on music, enters the Land of the Dead to find his great-great-grandfather.
review: A colorful, vibrant celebration of culture, family bonds, and remembrance.

description: In the distant future, a small waste-collecting robot inadvertently embarks on a space journey that will ultimately decide the fate of mankind.
review: Beautifully cinematic with an amazing first half that tells an emotional story without any dialogue.

description: On a journey to find the cure for a Tatarigami's curse, Ashitaka finds himself in the middle of a war between the forest gods and Tatara, a mining colony.
review: An epic, mature environmental fable with breathtaking world-building and rich themes.

description: After being kidnapped and imprisoned for fifteen years, Oh Dae-Su is released, only to find that he must find his captor in five days.
review: A dark, intense, and shocking thriller with one of the most brutal twist endings ever.

description: Eight years after the Joker's reign of anarchy, Batman, with the help of the enigmatic Catwoman, is forced from his exile to save Gotham City from Bane.
review: An epic, satisfying conclusion to Nolan's trilogy with massive scale and high stakes.

description: Decades after surviving an alien encounter, Ellen Ripley is sent back to the planet to establish contact with a terraforming colony.
review: A phenomenal shift from slow horror to intense, action-packed military sci-fi.

movie_title: Once Upon a Time in America
description: A former Prohibition-era Jewish gangster returns to the Lower East Side of Manhattan over thirty years later, where he must confront the ghosts and regrets of his past.
review: An elegant, melancholic, and epic look at crime and friendship over several decades.

description: The life, success and troubles of Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, as told by Antonio Salieri, the contemporary composer who was insanely jealous of his talent.
review: A lavish, energetic, and wonderfully theatrical examination of genius and jealousy.

description: In Nazi-occupied France during World War II, a plan to assassinate Nazi leaders by a group of Jewish U.S. soldiers coincides with a theater owner's vengeful plans.
review: Tarantino re-writes history with style. The opening farmhouse scene is pure masterclass suspense.

description: Will Hunting, a janitor at M.I.T., has a gift for mathematics, but needs help from a psychologist to find direction in his life.
review: A deeply moving script with spectacular chemistry between Robin Williams and Matt Damon.

description: The drug-induced utopias of four Coney Island people are shattered when their addictions control their lives.
review: A devastating, visually frenetic, and hauntingly powerful look at the descent into addiction.

description: The toys are mistakenly delivered to a day-care center rather than the attic right before Andy leaves for college.
review: An incredibly mature, emotional conclusion for the generation that grew up with Andy.

description: Scottish warrior William Wallace leads his countrymen in a rebellion to free his homeland from the tyranny of King Edward I of England.
review: An epic historical drama packed with passion, memorable speeches, and visceral battlefields.

description: When a simple jewelry heist goes horribly wrong, the surviving thieves suspect that one of them is a police informant.
review: Raw, talkative, and intensely structural. It put Tarantino on the map as a visionary.

movie_title: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope
description: Luke Skywalker joins forces with a Jedi Knight, a cocky pilot, a Wookiee and two droids to save the galaxy from the Empire's world-destroying battle station.
review: The space fantasy that changed the entertainment industry forever. Pure magical wonder.

description: Following the death of publishing tycoon Charles Foster Kane, reporters scramble to uncover the meaning of his final utterance: 'Rosebud'.
review: Revolutionary cinematography, lighting, and structure that set the standard for filmmaking.

description: After discovering a mysterious artifact buried beneath the Lunar surface, mankind sets off on a quest to find its origins with the help of supercomputer H.A.L. 9000.
review: A hypnotic, deeply philosophical sci-fi monument that expands the mind.

description: A former San Francisco police detective juggles his personal demons with his obsession for a hauntingly beautiful woman he is hired to tail.
review: A brilliant, psychological dive into obsession and illusion with gorgeous color design.

description: A teacher's lonely life Welles into a nightmare when he is wrongly accused of inappropriate behavior with a child.
review: A terrifyingly realistic look at how easily mass hysteria and rumors can destroy an innocent life.

description: Amélie is an innocent and naive girl in Paris with her own sense of justice. She decides to help those around her and, along the way, discovers love.
review: Whimsical, charming, and visually unique. It fills the heart with pure joy.

description: After John Nash, a brilliant mathematician, accepts secret work in cryptography, his life takes a turn for the nightmarish.
review: A highly engaging and respectful look at genius, schizophrenia, and enduring love.

description: An insurance salesman discovers his whole life is actually a reality TV show filmed by hidden cameras.
review: Prophetic, existential, and deeply touching. Jim Carrey gives his finest dramatic performance.

description: A chemistry teacher diagnosed with inoperable lung cancer turns to manufacturing and selling methamphetamine with a former student in order to secure his family's future.
review: The greatest television show ever written. Perfect character development from start to end.

description: The story of Easy Company of the U.S. Army 101st Airborne Division and their mission in World War II Europe, from Operation Overlord to V-J Day.
review: A flawless masterclass miniseries that honors the real soldiers with immense authenticity.

description: In April 1986, an explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant in the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics becomes one of the world's worst man-made catastrophes.
review: Terrifying, atmospheric, and highly gripping. A masterpiece detailing the high cost of lies.

description: The Baltimore drug scene, as seen through the eyes of drug dealers and law enforcement.
review: Incredibly realistic social commentary. It feels less like a show and more like real life.

description: Nine noble families fight for control over the lands of Westeros, while an ancient enemy returns after being dormant for millennia.
review: Despite the final seasons, the world-building, political intrigue, and scale were revolutionary.

description: New Jersey mob boss Tony Soprano deals with personal and professional problems in his home and business life that affect his mental state.
review: The show that launched the golden age of television. James Gandolfini is unmatched.

description: In a war-torn world of elemental magic, a young boy reawakens to undertake a dangerous mystic quest to fulfill his destiny as the Avatar.
review: Beautiful character arcs, deep philosophical lessons, and perfect pacing for all ages.

description: A modern update finds the famous sleuth and his doctor partner solving crime in 21st century London.
review: Smart, snappy writing with electric dynamic chemistry between Cumberbatch and Freeman.

description: A mockumentary on a group of typical office workers, where the workday consists of ego clashes, inappropriate behavior, and tedium.
review: Hilarious, comforting, and full of characters that feel like your actual friends.

description: The trials and tribulations of criminal lawyer Jimmy McGill in the years leading up to his fateful run-in with Walter White and Jesse Pinkman.
review: A masterfully slow-burning character study that rivals the greatness of Breaking Bad.

description: After his hometown is destroyed and his mother is killed, young Eren Jaeger vows to cleanse the earth of the giant humanoid Titans.
review: Mind-blowing plot twists, spectacular action sequences, and an intense, dark storyline.

description: Follows the personal and professional lives of six twenty to thirty-something-year-old friends living in Manhattan.
review: The ultimate comfort television show. Iconic jokes and comforting group dynamics.

description: When a young boy vanishes, a small town uncovers a mystery involving secret experiments, terrifying supernatural forces and one strange little girl.
review: A wonderful, nostalgic throw-back to 80s sci-fi and adventure films with great heart.

description: Various chronicles of deception, intrigue and murder in and around frozen Minnesota. Yet all of these tales mysteriously lead back one way or another to Fargo, North Dakota.
review: Quirky, dark, highly violent, and brilliantly written crime anthology series.

description: Seasonal anthology series in which police investigations unearth the personal and professional secrets of those involved, both within and outside the law.
review: Season 1 is arguably the finest single season of television ever broadcast. True poetry.

description: The Roy family is known for controlling the biggest media and entertainment company in the world. However, their world changes when their father steps down.
review: Shakespearian family drama mixed with sharp, brutal, and hilarious corporate dialogue.

description: The travels of a lone bounty hunter in the outer reaches of the galaxy, far from the authority of the New Republic.
review: It captured the classic Star Wars magic completely. Grogu and Mando are an iconic duo.

description: In the late 1970s two FBI agents expand criminal science by delving into the psychology of murder and awkwardly getting close to all too real monsters.
review: Fincher's meticulous direction creates an intensely chilling look at serial killer profiling.

description: A family saga with a supernatural twist, set in a German town, where the disappearance of two young children exposes the relationships among four families.
review: The most tightly written time-travel plot ever structured. It fits together flawlessly.

description: A gangster family epic set in 1900s Birmingham, England; centered on a gang who sew razor blades in the peaks of their caps, and their fierce boss Tommy Shelby.
review: Incredibly stylish cinematography, cool rock soundtracks, and a stellar Cillian Murphy.

description: The story follows the adventures of Aang, a young successor to a long line of Avatars, who must master all four elements and stop the Fire Nation.
review: Terrible adaptation. The acting was completely lifeless and butchered the masterpiece source material.

description: The young warrior Son Goku sets out on a quest, racing against time and the vengeful King Piccolo, to collect a set of seven magical orbs.
review: A complete insult to the franchise and the fans. Horrible choreography, awful visual effects.

description: Batman and Robin try to keep their relationship together even as they must stop Mr. Freeze and Poison Ivy from freezing Gotham City.
review: Cringey ice puns, cheesy over-the-top acting, and silly costumes ruined this entirely.

description: A shy woman, endowed with the speed, reflexes, and senses of a cat, walks a thin line between criminal and hero.
review: Awful storyline, terrible CGI, and a completely boring mess of an action movie.

description: Johnny is a successful banker who lives happily in a San Francisco townhouse with his fiancée, Lisa. One day, she inexplicably gets bored of him.
review: An absolute disaster of a script. The acting makes zero sense. Hilariously terrible.

description: When a freak hurricane swamps Los Angeles, nature's deadliest killer rules the sea, land, and air as thousands of sharks terrorize the waterlogged city.
review: Ridiculous plot, laughably bad practical effects, and terrible acting from start to finish.

description: A satirical comedy that parodies various Hollywood blockbusters, tracking four orphans through a mystical chocolate factory.
review: Completely unfunny, lazy references, and a brainless waste of time. Do not watch.

description: Over the course of a fateful night, a group of ridiculously attractive twenty-somethings must dodge various global threats.
review: Arguably one of the worst pieces of media ever put on film. Utterly agonizing trash.

description: Family guy Jack Sadelstein's life is turned upside down when his passive-aggressive twin sister Jill comes to visit.
review: Lazy, cheap gags and a completely uninspired script that wastes its high budget.

description: An ancient Egyptian princess is awakened from her crypt beneath the desert, bringing with her malevolence grown over millennia.
review: A boring, generic franchise starter with no real scares, charm, or narrative coherence.

description: Chief Brody's widow believes that a great white shark is deliberately hunting her family members across the ocean.
review: Stupid plot premise and a terrible mechanical shark that lacks any suspense or terror.

description: Tim Avery, an aspiring cartoonist, finds himself in a predicament when his dog stumbles upon the mask of Loki.
review: A creepy, unfunny sequel that completely ruins the energetic charm of Jim Carrey's original.

description: A paranormal investigator uncovers a terrifying secret portal leading to an ancient underworld hidden in the dark.
review: Incoherent editing, zero scary atmosphere, and widely considered a total failure.

description: In the year 3000, humanity has been conquered by an alien race known as the Psychlos. A rebellious human decides to fight back.
review: Terrible dialogue, ugly camera angles, and over-the-top acting make this painful to witness.

description: Larry Gigli is ordered to kidnap the psychologically challenged younger brother of a powerful federal prosecutor.
review: Zero chemistry, an embarrassing script, and a thoroughly unwatchable romantic comedy.

movie_title: Superman IV: The Quest for Peace
description: Superman crusades for nuclear disarmament and must square off against Lex Luthor's latest creation, Nuclear Man.
review: Cheap special effects and a messy storyline that completely grounded the legendary hero.

description: A small group of survivors are left behind after millions of people suddenly vanish from the surface of the Earth.
review: Dull pacing, preachy tone, and a complete waste of Nicolas Cage's acting talent.

description: In the eighteenth century, a half-human, half-vampire warrior escapes a freak show and sets out to kill her father.
review: Terrible action sequences, atrocious script edits, and absolutely wooden line deliveries.

description: Gene, a multi-expressional emoji, sets out on a journey to become a normal emoji like his parents.
review: A shameless, corporate product placement disguised as a shallow and unfunny film.

description: Literature student Anastasia Steele's life changes forever when she meets the handsome, yet tormented, billionaire Christian Grey.
review: Terrible narrative chemistry, boring execution, and an incredibly dull screenplay.
